steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a choice that makes a generalization about symbols on flags and then supports that generalization with evidence from the notes.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Many countries feature symbols on their flags, and the study of these designs is known as vexillology.: Makes a generalization but does not support it — it only defines vexillology rather than providing evidence about flag symbols. ✗
  • Choice B — Kazakhstan's flag includes an eagle, a symbol that is important to that country's national identity.: Gives a specific detail about one flag but never states a broader generalization. ✗
  • Choice C — Vexillology is the study of flags; accordingly, vexillologists are interested in flags from around the world.: Addresses studying flags rather than symbols on flags, so it does not meet the question's requirement. ✗
  • Choice D — The flags of some countries include symbols of animals; Kazakhstan's, for example, includes an eagle.: States a clear generalization about animal symbols on flags, then directly supports it with a concrete example from the notes. ✓
That is the correct answer.
